step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: Repeated administration of 2% dextran sulphate sodium (DSS) was used to induce chronic colitis in C57BL/6 mice, followed by a recovery period of 3 weeks
control: median disease activity index (DAI) of 0 IQR [0-0], cDSS: median DAI of 0.5 [0.5-0.88], P<0.0001

step_scores.fix_suggestion: No changes needed; fully consistent with source text.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: Behavioural testing was performed at baseline and after the recovery period and compared with control mice.
cDSS mice exhibited significantly reduced spontaneous activity compared with their baseline behaviour P=0.0084 and with the controls P=0.0186

step_scores.fix_suggestion: No changes needed; fully consistent with source text.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: RNA sequencing was performed of the distal colon and choroid plexus

step_scores.fix_suggestion: No changes needed; fully consistent with source text.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: weighted correlation network analysis (WGCNA) was applied.

WGCNA on the colon and the choroid plexus expression data revealed a consensus module of 123 co-expressed genes that differed significantly between the control and cDSS mice (P=0.00001721 ). These genes were implied in pathways of neutrophil and complement activation

step_scores.fix_suggestion: No changes needed; fully consistent with source text.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR) was used for the confirmation of the expression levels of genes identified in the WGCNA analysis.
qPCR on different brain regions (frontal cortex, hippocampus and choroid plexus) confirmed upregulation of inflammatory genes Lcn2, TNFa, S100A8 and S100A9

step_scores.fix_suggestion: No changes needed; fully consistent with source text.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: The number of microglia and their morphology were assessed by Iba1 immunofluorescence in a second experiment, also including acute colitis (aDSS), which was induced by 1 week of 2% DSS
Acute colitis was associated with activation of microglia in the prefrontal cortex and hippocampus, persisting in the prefrontal cortex of mice that recovered from chronic colitis

step_scores.fix_suggestion: No changes needed; fully consistent with source text.
